inputs.name: startDate
inputs.description: Starting date and time.

inputs.name: endDate
inputs.description: Ending date and time.

inputs.name: startDatePattern
inputs.description: Format in which the startDate parameter is to be specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: endDatePattern
inputs.description: Format in which the endDate parameter is to be specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

outputs.name: dateDifferenceSeconds
outputs.description: The difference between the startingDateTime and endingDateTime , truncated to the nearest whole number of seconds.

outputs.name: dateDifferenceMinutes
outputs.description: The difference between the startingDateTime and endingDateTime , truncated to the nearest whole number of minutes.

outputs.name: dateDifferenceHours
outputs.description: The difference between the startingDateTime and endingDateTime , truncated to the nearest whole number of hours.

outputs.name: dateDifferenceDays
outputs.description: The difference between the startingDateTime and endingDateTime , truncated to the nearest whole number of days.

inputs.name: startDate
inputs.description: Starting date to compare against endDate .

inputs.name: endDate
inputs.description: Ending date to compare against startDate .

inputs.name: startDatePattern
inputs.description: Format in which the startDate parameter is specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: endDatePattern
inputs.description: Format in which the endDate parameter is specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Checks whether startDate is before, the same, or after endDate .

inputs.name: nanoTime
inputs.description: Current time returned by the most precise system timer, in nanoseconds.

outputs.name: nanoTime
outputs.description: Current time returned by the most precise system timer, in nanoseconds.

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: Pattern representing the format in which you want the date returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols . If you do not specify pattern , dateBuild returns null. If pattern contains a time zone and timezone is not specified, the default time zone of IBM webMethods Integration Server is used.

inputs.name: year
inputs.description: Optional. The year expressed in yyyy or yy format (for example, 01 or 2001 ). If you do not specify year or you specify an invalid value, dateBuild uses the current year.

inputs.name: month
inputs.description: Optional. The month expressed as a number (for example, 1 for January, 2 for February). If you do not specify month or you specify an invalid value, dateBuild uses the current month.

inputs.name: dayofmonth
inputs.description: Optional. The day of the month expressed as a number (for example, 1 for the first day of the month, 2 for the second day of the month). If you do not specify dayofmonth or you specify an invalid value, dateBuild uses the current day.

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: Optional. Time zone in which you want the output date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone , the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: Optional. Locale in which the date is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002 , and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002 . If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server .

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: The date specified by year , month , and dayofmonth , in the format of pattern .

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: Pattern representing the format in which you want the time returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols . If you do not specify pattern , dateTimeBuild returns null. If pattern contains a time zone and the timezone parameter is not set, the time zone of Integration Server is used.

inputs.name: year
inputs.description: Optional. The year expressed in yyyy or yy format (for example, 01 or 2001 ). If you do not specify year or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses the current year.

inputs.name: month
inputs.description: Optional. The month expressed as a number (for example, 1 for January, 2 for February). If you do not specify month or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses the current month.

inputs.name: dayofmonth
inputs.description: Optional. The day of the month expressed as a number (for example, 1 for the first day of the month, 2 for the second day of the month). If you do not specify dayofmonth or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses the current day.

inputs.name: hour
inputs.description: Optional. The hour expressed as a number based on a 24-hour clock. For example, specify 0 for midnight, 2 for 2:00 A.M., and 14 for 2:00 P.M. If you do not specify hour or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses 0 as the hour value.

inputs.name: minute
inputs.description: Optional. Minutes expressed as a number. If you do not specify minute or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses 0 as the minute value.

inputs.name: second
inputs.description: Optional. Seconds expressed as a number. If you do not specify second or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses 0 as the second value.

inputs.name: millis
inputs.description: Optional. Milliseconds expressed as a number. If you do not specify millis or you specify an invalid value, dateTimeBuild uses 0 as the millis value.

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: Optional. Time zone in which you want the output date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone , the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: Optional. Locale in which the date is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002 , and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002 . If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server .

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Date and time in format of pattern .

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: Date/time that you want to convert. Important: If inString contains a character in the last position, that character is interpreted as 0. This can result in an inaccurate date. For information about invalid dates, see Notes on Invalid Dates .

inputs.name: currentPattern
inputs.description: Pattern string that describes the format of inString . For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: newPattern
inputs.description: Pattern string that describes the format in which you want inString returned. For pattern-string syntax, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the language and regional format of the input date string. For example, if the locale is en (for English), and the pattern is EEE d MMM yyyy , the input string should be Friday 23 August 2002 . If the locale is fr (for French), the input string should be vendredi 23 août 2002 . If the selected locale does not match the language of the input string, the service fails with a Failed to parse error. If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server .

inputs.name: lenient
inputs.description: Optional. A flag indicating whether Integration Server throws an exception if the inString value does not adhere to the format specified in currentPattern parameter. Set to: true to perform a lenient check. This is the default. In a lenient check, if the format of the date specified in the inString parameter does not match the format specified in the currentPattern parameter, Integration Server interprets and returns the date in the format specified in the currentPattern parameter. If the interpretation is incorrect, the service will return an invalid date. false to perform a strict check. In a strict check, the Integration Server throws an exception if the format of the date specified in the inString parameter does not match the format specified in the currentPattern parameter.

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: The date/time given by inString , in the format of newPattern .

inputs.name: nanoTime
inputs.description: Time in nanoseconds. If nanoTime is less than zero, then the service treats it as zero.

outputs.name: elapsedNanoTime
outputs.description: The difference between the current time in nanoseconds and nanoTime . If nanoTime is greater than the current nano time, the service returns zero.

outputs.name: elapsedNanoTimeStr
outputs.description: The difference between the current time in nanoseconds and nanoTime . The difference is expressed as a String, in this format: [years] [days] [hours] [minutes] [seconds] [millisec] [microsec] <nanosec> If nanoTime is greater than the current nano time, the service returns zero.

inputs.name: date
inputs.description: Optional. Date/time that you want to convert.

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: Pattern string that describes the format in which you want the date returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: Optional. Time zone in which you want the output date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone , the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: Optional. Locale in which the date is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002 , and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002 . If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server .

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: The date/time given by date in the format specified by pattern.

inputs.name: date
inputs.description: Current date.

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: Pattern representing the format in which you want the date returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: Optional. Time zone in which you want the output date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone , the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used. Alternatively, you can also specify the timezone as a full name. For more information, see Time Zones . For regions where Daylight Saving Time (DST) is observed, use the full name of the timezone for accurate time representation. For example, specifying the EST timezone code yields the current time as Eastern Standard Time without applying DST. To obtain Eastern Standard Time with DST adjustment, use America/New_York .

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: Optional. Locale in which the date is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002 , and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002 . If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server .

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Current date in the format specified by pattern .

inputs.name: startDate
inputs.description: Starting date and time.

inputs.name: endDate
inputs.description: Ending date and time.

inputs.name: startDatePattern
inputs.description: Format in which the startDate parameter is to be specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: endDatePattern
inputs.description: Format in which the endDate parameter is to be specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

outputs.name: workingDays
outputs.description: Number of days between startDate and endDate excluding weekends and holidays.

inputs.name: startDate
inputs.description: Starting date and time.

inputs.name: startDatePattern
inputs.description: Format in which the startDate parameter is specified (for example, yyyyMMdd HH:mm:ss.SSS). For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: endDatePattern
inputs.description: Optional. Pattern representing the format in which you want the endDate to be returned. For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols . If no endDatePattern is specified, the endDate will be returned in the format specified in the startDatePattern parameter.

inputs.name: addYears
inputs.description: Optional. Number of years to add to startDate . The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

inputs.name: addMonths
inputs.description: Optional. Number of months to add to startDate . The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

inputs.name: addDays
inputs.description: Optional. Number of days to add to startDate . The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

inputs.name: addHours
inputs.description: Optional. Number of hours to add to startDate . The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

inputs.name: addMinutes
inputs.description: Optional. Number of minutes to add to startDate . The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

inputs.name: addSeconds
inputs.description: Optional. Number of seconds to add to startDate . The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

inputs.name: addMilliSeconds
inputs.description: Optional. Number of milliseconds to add to startDate . The value must be an integer between -2147483648 and 2147483647.

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: Optional. Time zone in which you want the endDate to be expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone , the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property has not been set, GMT is used.

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: Optional. Locale in which the endDate is to be expressed. For example, if locale is en (for English), the pattern EEE d MMM yyyy will produce Friday 23 August 2002 , and the locale of fr (for French) will produce vendredi 23 août 2002 . If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server .

outputs.name: endDate
outputs.description: The end date and time, calculated by incrementing the startDate with the specified years, months, days, hours, minutes, seconds, and/or milliseconds. The endDate will be in the endDatePattern format, if specified. If no endDatePattern is specified or if blank spaces are specified as the value, the endDate will be returned in the format specified in the startDatePattern parameter.

inputs.name: inString
inputs.description: String Date or time in the string format that you want to convert to a Date object.

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: Pattern string that describes the format of inString . For pattern-string notation, see Pattern String Symbols .

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: Optional. Time zone in which you want the input date and time expressed. Specify a time zone code as shown in Time Zones (for example, EST for Eastern Standard Time). If you do not specify timezone , the value of the server's "user timezone" property is used. If this property is not set, GMT is used.

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the language and regional format of the input date string. For example, if the locale is en (for English), and the pattern is EEE d MMM yyyy , the input string should be Friday 23 August 2002 . If the locale is fr (for French), the input string should be vendredi 23 août 2002 . If the selected locale does not match the language of the input string, the service fails with a Failed to parse error. If locale is not specified, Integration Server uses the value of the watt.server.session.locale.ignore server configuration parameter to determine whether the locale is obtained from the session used by the client that invoked the service or if the locale is the locale of Integration Server .

outputs.name: date
outputs.description: The output date, which is of the java.util.Date wrapper type. This is dependent on the timezone specified. If nothing is specified, then the Integration Server timezone is used.
